metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a source-text scan (grep-style, not AST import-walk) to the extended `test_backend_neutrality.py` case that asserts `reconcile_check.py` contains no `adapters/jira/` path-string literal, complementing the AST adapter-import gate.
evidence: AC1: 'NO by-path load of a vendor sibling (adapters/jira/... path literal) — verified by the extended case in test_backend_neutrality.py'

Existing gate mechanism at test_backend_neutrality.py:193-204 uses ast.walk() checking only ast.Import and ast.ImportFrom node types — string arguments to _load_sibling() are Constant nodes, not Import nodes and would not be caught.
_load_sibling('outbound_fields', 'adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py') at reconcile_check.py:70 uses a path string, not an import statement — the AST gate would miss it if reintroduced.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 promises a gate that fails if `reconcile_check.py` re-grows a 'by-path vendor sibling load' (`adapters/jira/...` path literal), but the existing `_all_imported_modules` AST walker in `test_backend_neutrality.py` only detects `import X` / `from X import ...` AST nodes — it cannot detect `_load_sibling('key', 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` path-string arguments. Adding `reconcile_check.py` to `_ZERO_ADAPTER_IMPORT_SCALAR_CORE` alone won't verify the path-literal constraint; the gate needs a separate path-string denylist scan.
scenarios: Developer adds `_load_sibling('adf', 'adapters/jira/adf.py')` back to reconcile_check.py; existing AST import-walker passes because no 'import adapters.jira' statement exists; CI stays green despite the regression.
findings.suggested_fix: In the extended neutrality test for reconcile_check.py, add: `text = (_REC / 'reconcile_check.py').read_text(); assert 'adapters/jira/' not in text, 'reconcile_check.py contains a by-path vendor sibling load'`. This mirrors the existing denylist pattern used for vendor snapshot key literals at test_backend_neutrality.py:178-190.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the description-fit mechanism: specify using `InboundMapper.normalize_rich_text` to decode the remote ADF description and `fit_text_to_adf_limit` (via `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` or the equivalent non-warning path) for the local-side fit, matching exactly what `outbound_field_diff.diff_canonical_fields` does — NOT `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`, which is the send-path sanitizer and emits warnings.
evidence: Plan: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance'
jira_fields.py:158-175: `_sanitize_description` calls `_fit_description_to_adf_limit` and also calls `logger.warning(...)` on truncation — send-side side-effect
outbound_field_diff.py:275-278: the canonical differ calls `outbound_mapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})` to get the fitted description — no warning emitted
adapters/jira/backend.py:63-65: `map_fields_to_remote` description path calls `outbound_fields._load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit(value)` — pure fit, no warning
Reconcile-check is a read-only diagnostic (reconcile_check.py:8: 'pure (no I/O besides the return value)') — send-side warning logs break that contract
findings.finding: The plan specifies `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` for the local-description side of the description comparison, but this is the SEND-PATH sanitizer: `_sanitize_description` calls `fit_text_to_adf_limit` AND emits `logger.warning` on truncation. Using it in a read-only diagnostic tool introduces spurious send-side warning logs every time reconcile-check runs on a long description, and creates a different comparison surface than what `outbound_field_diff.diff_canonical_fields` uses (`outbound_mapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})`, which applies the ADF fit silently). The implementer will face an ambiguous choice between the two paths.
findings.location: Approach section — description canonicalization bullet: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text'
scenarios: A user runs `--mode reconcile-check` on a repo with a long (>32k-char) description; spurious 'description exceeded Jira limit' warnings appear in output even though no write is happening.

An implementer following the plan uses `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` while the inbound-differ comparison path uses `normalize_rich_text` + `fit_text_to_adf_limit` — edge-case comparison differences could cause reconcile-check to flag drift the differ would not.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description)' with 'compare `inbound_mapper.normalize_rich_text(local_val)` fitted via `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` against `canonical_remote["description"]` (already decoded by `map_remote_to_local`)' — this is exactly the `outbound_field_diff._text_matches(fitted, canonical_remote.get("description", ""))` path and stays warning-free.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Amend AC2 and the Testing section to acknowledge that `rebar-status:` annotation-label statuses are an exception to 'identical to today's decisions': the canonical mapper correctly resolves them, so a `blocked`/`cancelled` local ticket with matching annotation label should report IN-SYNC under the new approach (not the false discrepancy today's code would emit) — add this as an explicit edge case in the behavior tests.
evidence: inbound_fields.py:79-84: `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL = {'rebar-status:blocked': 'blocked', 'rebar-status:cancelled': 'cancelled'}` — annotation labels override Jira workflow status
inbound_fields.py:217-230: `_map_jira_to_local_fields` prefers annotation labels over raw status.name
reconcile_check.py:247: `jira_norm = of._extract_jira_field(jira_issue, field)` — reads only raw `status.name` (e.g. 'In Progress'), ignoring annotation labels
reconcile_check.py:248: `_values_match_with_mapping(field, local_val, jira_norm)` — compares `blocked` local against `In Progress` Jira → mismatch (current false discrepancy)
AC2: 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira' — this assertion is false for annotation-label statuses
findings.finding: AC2 states 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases … including the runny-lens-strafe live-shape regression.' However, for tickets with `rebar-status:blocked` or `rebar-status:cancelled` annotation labels, the NEW canonical approach will produce DIFFERENT (more correct) decisions than today: `inbound_fields._map_jira_to_local_fields` (ticket 929a) maps these annotation labels to `blocked`/`cancelled` local status, so `blocked` local == `blocked` canonical (no discrepancy). Today's code reads raw `status.name` via `_extract_jira_field` and `_values_match_with_mapping`, mapping `blocked` local → `In Progress` Jira — a false discrepancy. The AC's 'identical to today's' language creates a Testing trap where the characterization suite may be written to expect the old (wrong) behavior for annotation-label statuses.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; Testing — 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira'
scenarios: A ticket is locally `blocked`; Jira has `status.name='In Progress'` and label `rebar-status:blocked`. Today: reconcile-check falsely flags a status discrepancy. New approach: correctly sees no discrepancy. AC2's 'identical to today's' requirement would call the improvement a regression.
The Testing characterization suite is written expecting the OLD (false-positive) behavior for annotation-label cases, making the test suite reject the correct new behavior.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that description fitting in reconcile_check uses `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})` (as `outbound_field_diff.py:275-279` does) rather than `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` directly, so the ADF serialization+limit path is identical to the outbound differ's.
evidence: Plan text: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance'

Actual canonical differ in outbound_field_diff.py:275-279: `fitted = outbound_mapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc}, ticket=ticket).get('description', local_desc)` followed by `if not _text_matches(fitted, canonical_remote.get('description', ''))`
FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description in _backend.py:171 is a truncate/strip operation; it does NOT serialize to ADF nor apply the ADF character limit fitting that map_fields_to_remote performs

Ticket 625b description: 'Description parity: compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text' — same phrasing was used there too, but the implementation landed as map_fields_to_remote (Gerrit 931 comment confirms the implementation chose map_fields_to_remote path)
findings.finding: The plan asserts description comparison 'mirrors the differ's description parity rule' by using `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`, but the actual canonical differ (`outbound_field_diff.py:275-279`) fits descriptions via `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({'description': local_desc})` — which additionally serializes to ADF format — not via `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` alone. An executor following the plan literally will wire a different operation, breaking the stated decision-equivalence for ADF-heavy descriptions.
findings.location: Approach section — description bullet: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance, mirroring the differ's description parity rule [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]'
scenarios: A description that exceeds the ADF character limit: sanitize_description truncates differently from map_fields_to_remote's ADF serialization+fit, so reconcile_check would report a spurious discrepancy that the outbound differ would NOT emit an update for — the two tools disagree.
A plain-text description that needs no ADF fit: both paths agree, so the bug is silent in the common case.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`)' with 'fit the local description via `OutboundMapper.map_fields_to_remote({"description": local_val})` (identical to `outbound_field_diff.py:275-279`) and compare the fitted text against the mapper-decoded remote text with rstrip tolerance — do NOT use `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` directly, which skips ADF serialization.'

findings.finding: `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` is documented as a 'send-side only' sanitizer and emits a WARNING log on truncation; the inbound differ — the stated parity reference — uses `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit` directly (inbound_differ.py:210), NOT `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`. Using the send-side sanitizer in reconcile_check's comparison path would introduce spurious warning logs on every reconcile-check run for any description near the ADF limit, contradicting the 'no side-effects' expectation of a pure comparison.
findings.location: Approach section — 'description: compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text'
findings.suggested_fix: In the description comparison, call `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_issue)["description"]` for the remote side and use `fit_text_to_adf_limit(str(local_val or ""))` (via the same lazy adf loader the inbound differ uses) for the local side, preserving the rstrip tolerance — matching the inbound differ pattern at inbound_differ.py:210.

findings.finding: The description comparison plan — 'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance' — is an unverified behavioral equivalence claim. `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` is the send-side truncator (`_sanitize_description` → `fit_text_to_adf_limit`); after canonicalization via `map_remote_to_local`, the remote text is already plain text (ADF decoded, no fit applied). Today's code applies `fit_text_to_adf_limit` to the LOCAL value and compares against the ADF-decoded REMOTE value. The new path applies `sanitize_description` (= fit) to the local value and compares against the mapper-decoded remote value — which is structurally the same, BUT `sanitize_description` also enforces the description-must-be-str contract and emits a warning, making it a side-effectful call on the read path. The plan does not acknowledge this distinction.
findings.location: Approach section — 'description: compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text with the same rstrip tolerance'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `backend.sanitizer`'s underlying pure fit (e.g. call `adf.fit_text_to_adf_limit` via a port helper, or access it through the mapper's decoded output directly with rstrip) rather than `sanitize_description`. Alternatively, expose a separate `FieldSanitizer.fit_description(description: str) -> str` method that is pure (no warning, no raise) for comparison use.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2) to verify correct zero-discrepancy behavior for at least one synthetic non-Jira backend binding, ensuring the neutrality fix is tested and not just the Jira parity regression.
evidence: AC2: 'Discrepancy decisions are identical to today's for Jira across the Testing cases'
Context: 'its discrepancy scan would mis-flag every binding on a non-Jira backend, exactly the runny-lens-strafe failure mode'
Approach (Rejected alternative): 'reconcile_check is genuinely neutral core logic (a checker that must work for any backend)'
Testing section: all named cases (runny-lens-strafe, assignee, ADF, status) use Jira-shaped snapshots — no non-Jira backend test case
findings.finding: The plan's Context and Approach explicitly state that the PRIMARY motivation is fixing non-Jira backend correctness ('its discrepancy scan would mis-flag every binding on a non-Jira backend'), yet no Acceptance Criterion covers or verifies this non-Jira behavior — AC2 only requires 'identical to today's for Jira'. The non-Jira correctness fix is described work with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / AC2 vs. Context ('second-backend correctness hole') and Approach ('Rejected alternative')
scenarios: An implementer ships the change but accidentally still assumes Jira field shapes in one branch; no AC or test catches the regression for a non-Jira backend because none is specified.
The correctness improvement for non-Jira backends — the plan's primary justification — goes unverified at acceptance time.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2 with a second bullet: 'For a synthetic non-Jira backend (stub InboundMapper returning canonical local-vocabulary values), a fully-in-sync bound pair yields zero discrepancies — verified by an additional behavior test in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ using a mock backend.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one behavioral test using a minimal non-Jira stub `InboundMapper` (with distinct field shapes/names) to verify that a synced pair yields zero discrepancies and a divergent pair is correctly flagged — so the canonical-comparison path is exercised for the backend type the fix actually targets.
evidence: "Happy path: a fully-in-sync bound pair yields zero discrepancies on a live-shaped snapshot (nested status/priority objects, dict assignee, ADF description) — the runny-lens-strafe regression case, now via canonical mapping." — all specified shapes are Jira-shaped
"Gate: the extended neutrality case fails if reconcile_check.py re-grows an adapters.jira import or by-path vendor sibling load." — structural AST check, not behavioral

Absence: no Testing bullet or AC line names a non-Jira backend mapper (even a stub InboundMapper with distinct field shapes) to verify canonical comparison produces correct results for a second backend
The plan's own motivation states: reconcile_check must work for any backend; exempting it would leave a real second-backend correctness hole
findings.finding: The Testing section exercises only Jira-shaped payloads to verify behavioral correctness, but the core motivation of this story is fixing non-Jira backends (the runny-lens-strafe failure mode on non-Jira bindings). The extended neutrality gate is a structural/AST check (import scan), not a behavioral test. No test specifies a non-Jira mock backend where a synced pair produces zero discrepancies or a divergent pair is correctly flagged — so the actual bug fix (non-Jira correctness) is never behaviorally exercised.
scenarios: A future non-Jira backend is introduced; reconcile_check uses InboundMapper but the canonical comparison logic has a subtle bug (e.g., field-name mismatch in the mapper's output) — all existing tests pass (Jira shapes + neutrality gate) while every non-Jira binding is mis-flagged.
The fix is structurally correct (no adapter imports) but InboundMapper contract assumption is wrong for non-Jira shapes — only a behavioral test with a second backend shape would catch this.
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`, add a parameterized or separate test fixture that wires a trivial stub backend (e.g., a `StubInboundMapper` that maps `{"summary": ..., "assignee": ..., "status": {"name": ...}}` to local vocabulary with different key names than Jira), feeds reconcile_check a live-shaped snapshot from that backend, and asserts zero discrepancies for a synced pair and a reported discrepancy for a divergent field. Add this scenario to the Testing section and AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to Scope/AC that `reconcile_check.py`'s now-vestigial `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` are deleted (after canonicalization, status/priority compare as plain local-vocabulary equality).
evidence: reconcile_check.py:91-178 defines `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` (0→'Highest'…), `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_values_match_with_mapping` — all needed today only because comparison is in vendor shape.

inbound_fields.py:58-76 already defines `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY` and `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` (the inverse tables); after `map_remote_to_local` these are applied at the mapper boundary, making the reconcile_check copies redundant.
Scope lists only 'canonical comparison; drop vendor sibling loads' for reconcile_check.py — no mention of removing the private mapping tables.
AC lines confirm 'NO `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` import … NO by-path load' but say nothing about the local duplicate value constants.
findings.finding: After canonicalization via `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local`, status arrives as a local string and priority as a local int, making `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` in `reconcile_check.py` vestigial dead code with constants duplicating those already in `inbound_fields.py`. The plan's Scope and AC are silent on removing them.
findings.location: Approach section, status/priority bullet ('replacing `_extract_jira_field` + `_values_match_with_mapping`') and Scope / AC (no mention of removing the private value tables)
scenarios: Implementer canonicalizes the snapshot but leaves `_values_match_with_mapping` / `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` in place (they no longer run on the hot path), creating dead code with stale duplicates of `inbound_fields` constants.

A future change to the priority scale updates `inbound_fields._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_PRIORITY` but misses the dead copy in `reconcile_check.py`, which then silently mismaps if the dead code is ever re-activated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to the Approach: 'Delete `_values_match_with_mapping`, `_status_maps()`, `_PRIORITY_LOCAL_TO_JIRA`, and `_TYPE_LOCAL_TO_JIRA` from `reconcile_check.py`; after `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local` these are redundant — status and priority are already local-vocabulary values and compare with `==`.'

findings.finding: The plan specifies using `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description` (a send-side port, docstring: 'Defend the backend's hard limits on field values (send-side only)') to sanitize the local description before comparison, but the inbound_differ — the explicitly named model to mirror — calls `_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val)` directly (inbound_differ.py:210) without going through the FieldSanitizer port. Routing a read-side comparison through a send-side port is a golden-hammer misapplication: it forces a port dependency on reconcile_check for what is a pure in-memory text truncation, and it re-uses an abstraction outside its documented semantic scope.
findings.location: Approach section — description bullet: 'compare the port-sanitized local text (FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description' with '_load_adf().fit_text_to_adf_limit' in the description bullet of the Approach, matching inbound_differ.py:210 exactly. The mapper-decoded remote text is already plain-text after map_remote_to_local, so the comparison becomes: `fit_text_to_adf_limit(local_val).rstrip() == (mapped_remote_description or '').rstrip()` — no port injection needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare an explicit `depends_on` edge to `rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f` in this ticket's metadata (or add a 'Prerequisites' section), and enumerate the specific symbols/signatures consumed (`InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local`, `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`, `assignee_identity` field names) so the dependency contract is verifiable by inspection.
evidence: 'Apply the epic's canonical-comparison principle, reusing the machinery the prerequisite canonical-differ story establishes'
'neutral membership matching against the canonical `assignee_identity` dict `{display, email, account_id}` [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]'
'compare the port-sanitized local text (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text ... (mirroring the differ's description parity rule [rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f])'
ABSENCE: no explicit `depends_on` or `blocks` edge declared in the plan; reviewer cannot confirm edge from plan text alone
findings.finding: The plan relies on symbols from a prerequisite ticket — `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local`, `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`, and the canonical `assignee_identity` dict — cited inline as `[rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]`, but the plan does not declare a formal dependency edge (blocks/depends_on) and the reviewer cannot confirm from this plan alone that the prerequisite ticket's coverage affirmatively establishes these specific interfaces. Per the FAIL-CLOSED rule, the citation stands as an unchecked cross-ticket reference (item 9).
findings.location: Approach section — 'reusing the machinery the prerequisite canonical-differ story establishes'; inline `[rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f]` citations
scenarios: An executing agent writes reconcile_check.py against these symbols and the prerequisite ticket has not yet shipped them, or ships them under different names/signatures — the agent hits an import error or silent behavioral mismatch with no guidance.
The prerequisite ticket ships `InboundMapper` but with a different method name or signature than assumed, causing a silent divergence that AC2's behavior tests alone may not catch.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prerequisites' section: 'This story depends on `rebar:625b-9cd1-1ce9-4e5f` (canonical-differ story), which must ship: (1) `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local(snapshot_entry) -> LocalRecord`; (2) `FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description(text: str) -> str`; (3) `assignee_identity` dict with keys `display`, `email`, `account_id`. Confirm these signatures before implementation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the error-handling default for `map_remote_to_local` failures in the Approach section (e.g., 'if the mapper raises, treat the field as divergent and record the raw vendor value; do not abort the reconcile run') so the executor does not need to make an undocumented design choice.
evidence: 'Canonicalize the snapshot entry once per bound pair with the configured backend's `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local`'
'unmapped remote status compares as divergent' — only the no-mapping return value case is addressed
ABSENCE: no statement of what reconcile_check.py does if the mapper raises or returns an unexpected type
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the error-handling contract for `map_remote_to_local` failures during comparison (e.g., if the mapper raises an exception on a malformed snapshot entry, or returns an unexpected shape). The Testing section addresses 'unmapped remote status compares as divergent' but only for a well-formed no-mapping case, not for a mapper exception or None-return.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Canonicalize the snapshot entry once per bound pair with the configured backend's `InboundMapper.map_remote_to_local`'; Testing — 'unmapped remote status compares as divergent'
scenarios: A snapshot entry arrives with a field value the mapper cannot handle (raises ValueError/KeyError) — the executor must choose between: propagating the exception (reconciler aborts the run), catching and treating as divergent, or catching and skipping the field. Without guidance, the executor makes an undocumented choice that affects observable behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Approach: 'If `map_remote_to_local` raises or returns an unmappable sentinel, treat that field comparison as divergent (report raw `jira_value`; do not propagate the exception to the reconciler loop) — consistent with the existing `_values_match_with_mapping` fallback behavior.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `reconcile_check.py`'s neutral assignee membership matching will import and call the already-landed `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches(local_val, scalar, identity)` (preferred — avoids NIH) or express it locally; if local, document why `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches` is not the reuse vehicle.
evidence: outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches(local_val, scalar, identity) already exists (outbound_field_diff.py:70-80), performing exactly the neutral membership match described.
Plan says 'reusing the machinery the prerequisite canonical-differ story establishes' but never names `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches` as the reuse vehicle.

Only one production call site for `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches` today (inside `diff_canonical_fields`); re-implementing inline in reconcile_check adds duplication without crossing the Rule-of-Three threshold for a shared helper.
ABSENCE: no explicit import or reference to `outbound_field_diff` in the plan's scope or approach for reconcile_check.
findings.finding: The plan is ambiguous about whether 'neutral membership matching' will reuse the already-landed `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches(local_val, scalar, identity)` or re-implement the same logic inline in `reconcile_check.py`. The latter would be a single-call-site NIH rebuild of a function that already exists in core.
findings.location: Approach section, assignee bullet: 'neutral membership matching against the canonical `assignee_identity` dict … replacing the `_assignee_matches` call at `reconcile_check.py:236`'
scenarios: Implementer writes a new private `_assignee_matches`-equivalent inside `reconcile_check.py` (or `_compare_pair`) because the plan never says to import the existing one — two parallel implementations of the same three-way identity match must then be kept in sync.
A future bug fix to `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches` is not applied to the reconcile_check copy, causing divergence between the differ's and checker's assignee decisions.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, replace 'neutral membership matching against the canonical `assignee_identity` dict' with an explicit call reference: 'call `outbound_field_diff._assignee_matches(local_val, canonical_remote.get("assignee"), canonical_remote.get("assignee_identity"))`, the neutral helper already delivered by story 625b.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Scope whether `outbound_field_diff.py` helpers (`_assignee_matches`, `_text_matches`) will be reused by `reconcile_check.py`, and if so add `outbound_field_diff.py` to the Scope list; if the helpers will be re-implemented locally, document why.
evidence: outbound_field_diff.py:70: `def _assignee_matches(local_val: str, scalar: Any, identity: dict[str, Any] | None) -> bool:` — neutral membership matching, identical semantics to plan's described assignee comparison.
